source_file: THE REGULATION OF FINANCIAL PRIVACY- THE UNITED STATES VS EUROPE.txt
text:
3.2   Credit Markets and Information Sharing
The relationship between credit markets, information sharing and credit reporting has
been at the centre of several studies in the past (for an overview, see Table 6). We briefly
summarise the evidence to date and then present our own approach. One of the first
empirical surveys of information-sharing and credit markets is that of Pagano and
Jappelli (1993). The authors collected information on 14 OECD countries and divided

19
                                                 NICOLA JENTZSCH

them into two groups: one with widespread information-sharing and one with data
exchange on a smaller scale.17 The authors find that countries with credit bureaus exhibit
high mobility of consumers and deep consumer credit markets.

Table 6. Surveys of economic activity of credit bureaus

Jappelli          39-country comparison                           Theoretical predictions are consistent with the
 and                                                               data: Information sharing (IS) increases bank
 Pagano            Population: Public and private credit bureaus   lending, reduces credit risk and is negatively
 (2000a)           Methodology: Regression analysis                correlated with default rates (weak correlation)

Jappelli          17-country comparison (EU plus Turkey)          Privacy protection affects the amount of
 and                                                               information shared;
 Pagano            Population: Public and private credit bureaus   CBs originate from local lenders; consolidation
 (2000b)           Methodology: Description                        of the industry

Pagano and        14-country survey (OECD)                        IS is positively related to borrower mobility and
 Jappelli (1993)                                                   heterogeneity, size of credit market and advances
                   Population: Public and private credit bureaus   in IT;
                   Methodology: Regression analysis                IS increases lending volume if adverse selection
